name: Calcasieu Teachers and Employees
description_short: State-chartered credit union in Lake Charles, Louisiana, established in 1954, serving teachers and employees with $60.2M in assets and 4,800+ members.
description_long: Calcasieu Teachers And Employees Credit Union was established in 1954 and is headquartered in Lake Charles, Louisiana. Operating under a state charter, it has grown to serve over 4,800 loyal members in the region with a single branch location and 2 ATMs. The credit union maintains $60.2 million in assets and employs over 14 dedicated staff members as of 2026. The institution offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including checking accounts (Share Draft with 1,100+ accounts), savings accounts (Regular Savings with 5,100+ accounts), money market accounts (200+ accounts), auto loans for new and used vehicles, personal loans, mortgages (both fixed-rate 15-year and under options), and certificates of deposit. Members also have access to modern banking conveniences such as online bill pay, mobile payments, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and a mobile app available on both iOS and Android platforms. Key distinguishing features include the credit union's long operational history since 1954, its focus on serving teachers and employees in Louisiana, and its relatively lean but dedicated operational structure. The organization provides financial counseling services, drive-thru teller access, and multiple service status monitoring tools for members. The member base is concentrated within Louisiana, with the institution operating under state rather than federal charter. As a small community credit union, Calcasieu Teachers And Employees CU serves a specific regional membership focused on educators and employees in the Lake Charles area. Members should be aware that the institution operates a single physical branch, which may limit in-person service accessibility for those outside the immediate Lake Charles area. The modest asset base and branch count compared to larger regional or national credit unions means more limited physical infrastructure, though digital banking options partially offset this limitation.
pros: Established in 1954 with over 70 years of operational history and stability
Maintains $60.2 million in assets, indicating reasonable financial strength for a community credit union
Offers comprehensive loan products including auto loans, personal loans, and mortgages
Provides modern digital banking including mobile app (iOS/Android), online bill pay, and remote deposit capture
Member-focused with 4,800+ members and financial counseling services available
Multiple account types including Regular Savings, Share Draft, Money Market, and Share Certificates
Drive-thru teller service and ATM access (2 ATMs on network)
cons: Only 1 physical branch location in Lake Charles, limiting accessibility for out-of-area members
Appears to be membership-restricted to specific employee/teacher groups in Louisiana
Currently showing both lobby and drive-thru as closed on the website (status unclear if permanent or temporary)
No reviews currently posted on the profile, limiting peer feedback for prospective members
Limited ATM network with only 2 ATMs versus larger credit unions' broader access
best_for: Teachers and educators in the Calcasieu Parish or Lake Charles area
Current and former employees of eligible Louisiana organizations serving in the credit union's membership field
Louisiana residents seeking a community-focused credit union with long operational history
Members comfortable with limited physical branch presence but wanting modern digital banking access
diagnosis: Calcasieu Teachers And Employees CU is best for Louisiana-based teachers and eligible employees seeking a community credit union with comprehensive loan and deposit services, modern digital banking, and a 70-year track record. The primary caveat is the single-branch operating model and apparent membership restrictions, making it suitable mainly for those within Lake Charles or able to conduct most banking digitally.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
